introDesc: A mod management tool designed for Nintendo Switch. Built on a brand new architecture with a complete rewrite and expanded features. This project is open-source and permanently free, with no paid content of any kind. Due to limited personal development capacity, the collection and maintenance of online mod resources depends on community participation and contributions.
introHighlightsDesc:
· Modern and clean UI design
· Deep Chinese language support
· Simple and intuitive workflow
· Supports both ZIP and folder mod formats
· Automatically converts pchtxt patches to IPS patches
· Smart detection of non-standard mod structures
· Strict multi-mod file conflict handling
· One-click disable or restore all mods
· Quickly add games from installed game list
· Add mods via FTP and MTP
· Built-in online mod store
· Fast and efficient search
· Rich mod customization options
· Light and dark themes with system-following option
· In-app update check and download
disclaimerSoftwareDesc: This project is maintained by one person who is not a professional developer. There is no guarantee that the software is free of critical bugs. Any save data corruption or mod file damage that may result is the user's own responsibility.
disclaimerServiceDesc: The server and website are also maintained by the author alone, purely out of passion, with no guarantee of long-term operation. However, this project will always remain free. If maintenance becomes impossible, the server and data will be handed over to someone willing to continue.
disclaimerCopyrightDesc: If any user-uploaded mod infringes on the original author's rights, please contact me and it will be removed immediately.
donateStatementDesc: Thank you to every user who supports this project. This is a completely free personal project — all features are available to all users equally. Donations do not unlock any extra features, permissions, or special treatment. Please donate only if your financial situation allows. If you currently lack a stable income or are under financial pressure, please prioritize your personal and family expenses. Your usage and recognition is the greatest support for this project.
feedbackInfoDesc: When submitting bug reports, please use GitHub Issues as the preferred channel, and provide complete and accurate information. Due to limited personal capacity, reports that are incomplete or vaguely described will not be actively followed up with repeated clarification requests. Thank you for your understanding.
feedbackRequireDesc:
· Atmosphere and system firmware version
· Detailed steps that trigger the issue
· Whether the issue is consistently reproducible
· If only triggered by a specific file, please provide it
· Any other information helpful for diagnosing the issue
guideIntroDesc: Below is a basic usage overview. For more detailed tutorials with screenshots and videos, scan the QR codes at the bottom.
guideAddGameDesc: Press  on the home page to open the menu, select "Manage Games" → "Add Game", then choose a game from the installed games list.
guideTransferDesc: Transfer mod files to the transit area via MTP (USB wired) or FTP (Wi-Fi wireless). Both ZIP and folder formats are supported. Transfer options can be found in the menu on the Add Game and Mod List pages.
guideAddModDesc: After transfer, tap the target game on the "Add Game" page to select mods from the transit area. You can also add mods from the "Add Mod" option in the Mod List page menu.
guideInstallDesc: In the mod list, tap a mod card to install or uninstall it. File conflicts between mods are automatically detected during installation.
guideOnlineDesc: Press  on the home page or mod list page to open the mod store. Browse and download online mods, which are automatically added to the game after download.
faqTransfer: How to transfer mod files to the console
faqTransferDesc: The software supports two transfer methods, accessible from the menu on the Add Game or Mod List pages via . MTP requires a USB cable connection to your computer — a storage device named "Add Mod" will appear on your PC, simply drag files into it. FTP requires the console and computer to be on the same Wi-Fi network — the app will display an FTP address to connect with an FTP client. ZIP files are recommended for MTP high-speed transfer, while folder-based mods work best with FTP using multi-threaded mode. Transferred files are stored in the transit area, awaiting assignment to a game.
faqUpload: How to upload mods to the store
faqUploadDesc: Mod uploads must be done via the web. Press  on the home page to open the store menu, select "View Website" and scan the QR code to access the web store. The web interface provides mod browsing and uploading. When uploading, select the corresponding game and fill in basic mod information. Uploaded mods will appear in the online store after review. Please obtain the original author's permission before uploading. Uploading paid, pornographic, or sensitive content is strictly prohibited and will result in a permanent ban.
faqTransitEmptyDesc: The transit area is a temporary storage location for mod files, located at /mods2/!temp_mods/ on the SD card. Files transferred via MTP or FTP are stored here first. This message means no mod files are in transit. Please upload mods via MTP or FTP from the menu first, then tap a game card to select and add mods.
faqNetworkDesc: Store browsing, mod downloads, and online game name retrieval all require a network connection. If you see network-related errors, verify that your console is connected to Wi-Fi. If the network is fine but access still fails, the server may be temporarily unavailable. Also ensure your Switch has been time-synced, as incorrect time can cause network request failures.
faqInvalidModDesc: This message means no valid mod file structure was found in the folder or ZIP archive. A valid mod must contain at least one of: romfs, exefs, cheats, exefs_patches, romfs.bin, or a pchtxt patch file.
faqConflictDesc: When installing a mod, the software checks if any files it modifies are already occupied by other mods. If two mods modify the same file with different content, a conflict is detected and the conflicting mod name is shown. You need to uninstall the conflicting mod first. Files with identical content across multiple mods do not trigger conflicts — the software uses reference counting to manage shared files safely.
faqRemoveGame: Cannot remove game with installed mods
faqRemoveGameDesc: To prevent installed mod files from being left behind in system directories, all mods must be uninstalled before removing a game. Go to the game's mod list, uninstall each installed mod, then remove the game. After removal, mod files are automatically moved to the transit area for future use.
faqDuplicateTidDesc: TID is a unique identifier for each game. The software uses TID to manage mod files. If the same TID exists in different SD card directories, it causes data management conflicts. Duplicate games are highlighted in red on the home page. Please manually merge folders with the same TID into one, ensuring each TID maps to a single directory.
faqForceCleanDesc: Force clean repairs reference count file corruption caused by abnormal interruptions (e.g., sleep, forced shutdown) during mod installation or uninstallation. Corrupted reference counts can cause conflict detection errors or incomplete uninstalls. After force cleaning, all installed mod files in the game directory are deleted (including manually installed mods), and mods need to be reinstalled. This operation cannot be undone — use it only when the above issues are confirmed.
faqCustomDesc: Due to Switch input limitations, editing mod names, descriptions, and version numbers on the console is indeed inconvenient. However, these customization features are optional extensions — they do not affect core operations like installing and uninstalling mods. If you find it cumbersome, feel free to use only the core features. But please do not use this as grounds for thoughtless criticism, or you will likely receive a sarcastic response from the author.
faqModFormat: What are ZIP type and folder type mods
faqModFormatDesc:
The software supports two mod types. The built-in smart recognition algorithm can automatically parse mod directory structures, so files do not need to follow a strict standard layout.
· ZIP type: mod files packaged as a ZIP archive
· Folder type: mod files stored directly as a directory
